content:
# Numbers Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 150–152; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 113–114 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_004_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_004_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_004_page_003.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_004_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_004_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE SERVICE OF THE LEVITICAL CLANS:** The Leningrad Codex continues on pages 150–152 with the detailed assignment of Tabernacle duties to the three Levitical clans: the Kohathites (vv. 1–20), the Gershonites (vv. 21–28), and the Merarites (vv. 29–33), followed by the census of those eligible for service (vv. 34–49). The age range for active Tabernacle service is specified as thirty to fifty years (מִבֶּן שְׁלֹשִׁים שָׁנָה, mi-ben sheloshim shanah), a narrower band than the one-month-and-upward count of chapter 3. The chapter is dominated by the verb עָבַד ('avad, "to serve/work") and the noun עֲבֹדָה ('avodah, "service/work"), which together appear over thirty times — underscoring that Levitical calling is defined by labor, not status. The elaborate wrapping and covering procedures for the holy vessels (vv. 5–15) reveal a theology of graduated holiness: only Aaron and his sons may see and touch the most sacred objects, while the Kohathites carry but never look upon them on pain of death. The Vaticanus LXX (pp. 113–114) follows the MT with close fidelity, rendering YHWH as κύριος throughout, and occasionally using ὑακίνθινον ("hyacinth-colored") for תְּכֵלֶת (tekhelet, "blue/violet").

---

## The Duties of the Kohathites (vv. 1–20)

**1** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh] and-unto [ve-el] Aaron [Aharon], saying [le'mor]:

**2** "Take [naso'] the-sum of [et-ro'sh] the-sons of [benei] Kohath [Qehat] from-among [mi-tokh] the-sons of [benei] Levi [Levi], by-their-families [le-mishpekhotam], by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam],

> **Key terms:** נָשֹׂא אֶת־רֹאשׁ (naso' et-ro'sh) = "lift the head" = "take a census/sum." The identical idiom used in 1:2 for the general census is now applied to the Kohathites specifically. **LXX:** Λαβὲ τὸ κεφάλαιον τῶν υἱῶν Κααθ — κεφάλαιον ("sum/head-count") for רֹאשׁ.

**3** from-a-son of [mi-ben] thirty [sheloshim] year [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah] and-unto [ve-ad] a-son of [ben] fifty [khamishim] year [shanah], all [kol] who-come [ha-ba'] to-the-host [la-tsava'], to-do [la'asot] work [melakhah] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed].

> **Key terms:** לַצָּבָא (la-tsava') = "to the host/service-corps" — the military metaphor frames Tabernacle work as a kind of campaign. מְלָאכָה (melakhah) = "work/skilled labor," distinct from עֲבֹדָה ('avodah). **LXX:** ἀπὸ εἰκοσιπέντε ἐτῶν ("from twenty-five years") — the LXX here reads twenty-five rather than the MT's thirty, a well-known divergence also attested in Num 8:24. The Leningrad Codex clearly reads שְׁלֹשִׁים (thirty).

**4** This [zo't] [is] the-service of [avodat] the-sons of [benei] Kohath [Qehat] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed]: the-holy-of-holies [qodesh ha-qodashim].

> **Key terms:** קֹדֶשׁ הַקֳּדָשִׁים (qodesh ha-qodashim) = "the holy of holies" / "the most holy things." Here it refers not to the inner chamber itself but to the most sacred objects that the Kohathites are charged to transport. **LXX:** τὸ ἅγιον τῶν ἁγίων.

**5** And-shall-come [u-va'] Aaron [Aharon] and-his-sons [u-vanav] when-the-camp [be-neso'a ha-makhaneh] sets-out, and-they-shall-take-down [ve-horidu] the-curtain of [et parokhet] the-screen [ha-masakh], and-they-shall-cover [ve-khissu] with-it [bah] the-ark of [et aron] the-testimony [ha-edut].

> **Key terms:** פָּרֹכֶת הַמָּסָךְ (parokhet ha-masakh) = "the curtain of the screen" — the veil separating the Holy of Holies from the Holy Place (Exod 26:31–33). Only the priests handle this veil; it becomes the first wrapping for the Ark. **LXX:** τὸ καταπέτασμα τὸ συσκιάζον ("the veil that overshadows") — a distinctive rendering.

**6** And-they-shall-put [ve-natenu] upon-it [alav] a-covering of [kesu'i] skin of [or] takhash [takhash], and-they-shall-spread [u-farsu] a-garment [beged] all of [kelil] blue [tekhelet] from-above [mi-lema'lah], and-they-shall-set [ve-samu] its-poles [baddav].

> **Key terms:** תַּחַשׁ (takhash) = a type of hide or skin; the exact animal remains debated (dugong? fine leather? a color term?). תְּכֵלֶת (tekhelet) = "blue/violet" — the prestigious dye associated with royalty and holiness. כְּלִיל (kelil) = "entire/whole" — the cloth is entirely blue. **LXX:** δέρμα ὑακίνθινον ("hyacinth-colored skin") conflates the takhash covering and blue cloth into one phrase, though the MT distinguishes them as two separate layers.

**7** And-upon [ve-al] the-table of [shulkhan] the-face [ha-panim] they-shall-spread [yifresu] a-garment of [beged] blue [tekhelet], and-they-shall-put [ve-natenu] upon-it [alav] the-dishes [et-ha-qe'arot], and-the-cups [ve-et-ha-kappot], and-the-bowls [ve-et-ha-menaqqi'yot], and-the-jugs of [ve-et-qesvot] the-drink-offering [ha-nasekh]; and-the-bread of [ve-lekhem] continuity [ha-tamid] shall-be [yihyeh] upon-it [alav].

> **Key terms:** שֻׁלְחַן הַפָּנִים (shulkhan ha-panim) = "the table of the face/presence" = the table of showbread (Exod 25:23–30). לֶחֶם הַתָּמִיד (lekhem ha-tamid) = "the bread of continuity/perpetuity" — the twelve loaves that were always present. **LXX:** τὴν τράπεζαν τὴν προκειμένην ("the table that is set before") — a functional rendering of שלחן הפנים.

**8** And-they-shall-spread [u-farsu] upon-them [alehem] a-garment of [beged] scarlet of [sheni tola'at] the-worm [tola'at], and-they-shall-cover [ve-khissu] it [oto] with-a-covering of [be-mikhseh] skin of [or] takhash [takhash], and-they-shall-set [ve-samu] its-poles [et-baddav].

> **Key terms:** שְׁנִי תוֹלַעַת (sheni tola'at) = "scarlet of the worm/crimson" — the scarlet dye derived from the coccus insect. The layering order for the table is: blue cloth, then vessels and bread, then scarlet cloth over all, then takhash skin outermost. **LXX:** ἱμάτιον κόκκινον ("scarlet garment").

**9** And-they-shall-take [ve-laqkhu] a-garment of [beged] blue [tekhelet], and-they-shall-cover [ve-khissu] the-lampstand of [et-menorat] the-light [ha-ma'or], and-its-lamps [ve-et-neroteyha], and-its-tongs [u-malqakheyha], and-its-fire-pans [u-makhtoteyha], and-all [ve-et-kol] the-vessels of [kelei] the-oil [ha-shemen] with-which [asher] they-serve [yesharetu] to-it [lah].

> **Key terms:** מְנֹרַת הַמָּאוֹר (menorat ha-ma'or) = "the lampstand of the light" — the golden seven-branched menorah (Exod 25:31–40). מַלְקָחֶיהָ (malqakheyha) = "its tongs/snuffers." מַחְתֹּתֶיהָ (makhtoteyha) = "its fire-pans/censers." **LXX:** τὴν λυχνίαν τὴν φωτίζουσαν ("the lampstand that gives light").

**10** And-they-shall-put [ve-natenu] it [otah] and-all [ve-et-kol] its-vessels [keleha] unto [el] a-covering of [mikhseh] skin of [or] takhash [takhash], and-they-shall-put [ve-natenu] [it] upon [al] the-carrying-frame [ha-mot].

> **Key terms:** הַמּוֹט (ha-mot) = "the carrying-frame/pole" — a single pole or bar used to carry the wrapped lampstand assembly. The lampstand alone is placed on a mot rather than having permanent poles (בַּדִּים, baddim) like the Ark and table. **LXX:** ἐπὶ ἀναφορέων ("upon carrying-poles").

**11** And-upon [ve-al] the-altar of [mizbekh] the-gold [ha-zahav] they-shall-spread [yifresu] a-garment of [beged] blue [tekhelet], and-they-shall-cover [ve-khissu] it [oto] with-a-covering of [be-mikhseh] skin of [or] takhash [takhash], and-they-shall-set [ve-samu] its-poles [et-baddav].

> **Key terms:** מִזְבַּח הַזָּהָב (mizbekh ha-zahav) = "the altar of gold" = the incense altar (Exod 30:1–10), distinguished from the bronze altar of burnt offering in the courtyard.

**12** And-they-shall-take [ve-laqkhu] all [et-kol] the-vessels of [kelei] the-ministry [ha-sharet] with-which [asher] they-minister [yesharetu] in-the-holy-place [ba-qodesh], and-they-shall-put [ve-natenu] [them] unto [el] a-garment of [beged] blue [tekhelet], and-they-shall-cover [ve-khissu] them [otam] with-a-covering of [be-mikhseh] skin of [or] takhash [takhash], and-they-shall-put [ve-natenu] [them] upon [al] the-carrying-frame [ha-mot].

> **Key terms:** כְּלֵי הַשָּׁרֵת (kelei ha-sharet) = "the vessels of ministry" — all remaining holy implements used in the sanctuary service.

**13** And-they-shall-remove-the-ashes of [ve-dishenu] the-altar [et-ha-mizbe'akh], and-they-shall-spread [u-farsu] upon-it [alav] a-garment of [beged] purple [argaman].

> **Key terms:** וְדִשְּׁנוּ (ve-dishenu) = "and they shall remove the fatty ashes" — from דשׁן, "fat/ashes." This is the bronze altar of burnt offering. אַרְגָּמָן (argaman) = "purple" — royal purple dye. Note: only the bronze altar is covered with purple; the other objects receive blue. **LXX:** καὶ τὸν καλυπτῆρα ἐπιθήσουσιν ἐπ᾽ αὐτό — renders the covering procedure but diverges somewhat from the MT.

**14** And-they-shall-put [ve-natenu] upon-it [alav] all [et-kol] its-vessels [kelav] with-which [asher] they-minister [yesharetu] upon-it [alav]: the-fire-pans [et-ha-makhtot], the-forks [ha-mizlagot], and-the-shovels [ve-ha-ya'im], and-the-basins [ve-ha-mizraqot] — all [kol] the-vessels of [kelei] the-altar [ha-mizbe'akh]; and-they-shall-spread [u-farsu] upon-it [alav] a-covering of [mikhseh] skin of [or] takhash [takhash], and-they-shall-set [ve-samu] its-poles [et-baddav].

> **Key terms:** מַחְתֹּת (makhtot) = "fire-pans/censers." מִזְלָגֹת (mizlagot) = "forks" (three-pronged). יָעִים (ya'im) = "shovels" (for ashes). מִזְרָקֹת (mizraqot) = "basins/bowls" (for sprinkling blood). **LXX:** lists the same implements: τὰ πυρεῖα, τὰς κρεάγρας, τὰς φιάλας.

**15** And-shall-finish [ve-khillah] Aaron [Aharon] and-his-sons [u-vanav] to-cover [le-khassot] the-holy-things [et-ha-qodesh] and-all [ve-et-kol] the-vessels of [kelei] the-holy-things [ha-qodesh] when-the-camp [be-neso'a ha-makhaneh] sets-out, and-after-thus [ve-akharei-khen] shall-come [yavo'u] the-sons of [benei] Kohath [Qehat] to-carry [la-se't], and-they-shall-not [ve-lo'] touch [yigge'u] unto [el] the-holy-things [ha-qodesh] and-they-shall-die [va-metu]. These [elleh] [are] the-carrying-load of [massa'] the-sons of [benei] Kohath [Qehat] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed].

> **Key terms:** וְלֹא־יִגְּעוּ אֶל־הַקֹּדֶשׁ וָמֵתוּ (ve-lo' yigge'u el-ha-qodesh va-metu) = "and they shall not touch the holy things and-they-shall-die" — the death warning for unauthorized contact with sacred objects. מַשָּׂא (massa') = "burden/carrying-load" — from נשׂא, "to lift/carry," the same root used for the census in v. 2. **LXX:** καὶ οὐχ ἅψονται τῶν ἁγίων, ἵνα μὴ ἀποθάνωσιν ("and they shall not touch the holy things, lest they die") — the Greek ἵνα μή softens the blunt Hebrew statement into a purpose clause.

**16** And-the-oversight of [u-fequddat] Eleazar [El'azar] the-son of [ben] Aaron [Aharon] the-priest [ha-kohen]: the-oil of [shemen] the-light [ha-ma'or], and-the-incense of [u-qetoret] the-spices [ha-sammim], and-the-grain-offering of [u-minkhat] the-continuity [ha-tamid], and-the-oil of [ve-shemen] the-anointing [ha-mishkhah] — the-oversight of [peqqduat] all [kol] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan] and-all [ve-khol] that [asher] [is] in-it [bo], in-the-holy-place [ba-qodesh] and-in-its-vessels [u-ve-khelav].

> **Key terms:** פְּקֻדַּת (pequddat) = "oversight/charge/appointed responsibility" — from פקד, "to appoint/visit/number." Eleazar has personal responsibility over the oil, incense, daily grain offering, anointing oil, and indeed the entire Tabernacle and its contents. מִנְחַת הַתָּמִיד (minkhat ha-tamid) = "the grain offering of continuity" — the daily meal offering. **LXX:** ἡ ἐπισκοπή Ελεαζαρ ("the oversight of Eleazar") — ἐπισκοπή for פקדת.

**17** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh] and-unto [ve-el] Aaron [Aharon], saying [le'mor]:

**18** "Do-not [al] cut-off [takhrithu] the-tribe of [et-shevet] the-families of [mishpekhot] the-Kohathites [ha-Qehati] from-among [mi-tokh] the-Levites [ha-Levi'im].

> **Key terms:** אַל־תַּכְרִיתוּ (al-takhrithu) = "do not cut off/exterminate" — a severe warning that carelessness with the holy objects could result in the annihilation of the entire Kohathite clan. **LXX:** μὴ ὀλεθρεύσητε ("do not destroy").

**19** And-this [ve-zo't] do [asu] to-them [lahem] and-they-shall-live [ve-khayyu] and-not [ve-lo'] die [yamutu] in-their-approaching [be-gishtam] the-holy-of-holies [et-qodesh ha-qodashim]: Aaron [Aharon] and-his-sons [u-vanav] shall-come [yavo'u] and-they-shall-appoint [ve-samu] them [otam], each-man [ish] upon [al] his-service [avodato] and-upon [ve-el] his-carrying-load [massa'o].

> **Key terms:** וְחָיוּ וְלֹא יָמֻתוּ (ve-khayyu ve-lo' yamutu) = "and they shall live and not die" — the positive counterpart to the death warning. The procedure that preserves their lives is priestly supervision of the wrapping process. **LXX:** ζήσονται καὶ οὐ μὴ ἀποθάνωσιν ("they shall live and shall by no means die").

**20** And-they-shall-not [ve-lo'] come [yavo'u] to-see [lir'ot] as-the-swallowing of [ke-valla'] the-holy-things [et-ha-qodesh] and-they-shall-die [va-metu]."

> **Key terms:** כְּבַלַּע (ke-valla') = "as the swallowing/covering" — a hapax-like usage; the root בלע normally means "to swallow," but here it is understood as "for an instant/moment" or "the moment of covering" — the brief instant when the holy objects are exposed during wrapping. The Kohathites must not see the sacred vessels even for a moment (כְּבַלַּע) lest they die. **LXX:** οὐκ ὄψονται... ἐξαίφνης ("they shall not see... suddenly") — renders the temporal sense of בלע.

---

## The Duties of the Gershonites (vv. 21–28)

**21** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**22** "Take [naso'] the-sum of [et-ro'sh] the-sons of [benei] Gershon [Gershon] also [gam] them [hem], by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam], by-their-families [le-mishpekhotam].

**23** From-a-son of [mi-ben] thirty [sheloshim] year [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah] unto [ad] a-son of [ben] fifty [khamishim] year [shanah] you-shall-number [tifqod] them [otam], all [kol] who-come [ha-ba'] to-serve [la-tsevo'] a-service [tsava'] to-do [la'avod] work [avodah] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed].

> **Key terms:** לִצְבֹא צָבָא (li-tsevo' tsava') = "to serve a service/to wage a campaign" — the cognate accusative reinforces the military metaphor for sacred labor.

**24** This [zo't] [is] the-service of [avodat] the-families of [mishpekhot] the-Gershonites [ha-Gershunni], to-serve [la'avod] and-to-carry [u-le-massa']:

**25** And-they-shall-carry [ve-nas'u] the-curtains of [et-yeri'ot] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan] and-the-tent of [ve-et-ohel] meeting [mo'ed], its-covering [mikhsehu] and-the-covering of [u-mikhseh] the-takhash [ha-takhash] that [asher] [is] upon-it [alav] from-above [mi-lema'lah], and-the-screen of [ve-et-masakh] the-entrance of [petakh] the-tent of [ohel] meeting [mo'ed],

**26** and-the-hangings of [ve-et-qal'ei] the-court [he-khatser] and-the-screen of [ve-et-masakh] the-entrance of [petakh] the-gate of [sha'ar] the-court [he-khatser] that [asher] [is] upon [al] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan] and-upon [ve-al] the-altar [ha-mizbe'akh] all-around [saviv], and-their-cords [ve-et-metareihem] and-all [ve-et-kol] the-vessels of [kelei] their-service [avodatam], and-all [ve-et-kol] that [asher] shall-be-done [ye'aseh] for-them [lahem] — and-they-shall-serve [ve-avadu].

> **Key terms:** יְרִיעֹת (yeri'ot) = "curtains" — the inner linen curtains of the Tabernacle. מִכְסֵהוּ (mikhsehu) = "its covering" — the goat-hair tent over the linen curtains. קַלְעֵי (qal'ei) = "hangings" — the linen screens forming the courtyard enclosure. מֵיתְרֵיהֶם (metareihem) = "their cords/tent-ropes." The Gershonites carry all the soft/textile components. **LXX:** τὰς δέρρεις τῆς σκηνῆς ("the skins/curtains of the tent").

**27** By [al] the-mouth of [pi] Aaron [Aharon] and-his-sons [u-vanav] shall-be [yihyeh] all [kol] the-service of [avodat] the-sons of [benei] the-Gershonites [ha-Gershunni], in-all [le-khol] their-carrying-load [massa'am] and-in-all [u-le-khol] their-service [avodatam]; and-you-shall-appoint [u-feqadtem] upon-them [alehem] in-charge [be-mishmeret] all [et-kol] their-carrying-load [massa'am].

> **Key terms:** עַל־פִּי (al-pi) = "by the mouth of" = "according to the command of." The Gershonites serve under the direction of Aaron's sons. **LXX:** κατὰ στόμα Ααρων ("according to the mouth of Aaron").

**28** This [zo't] [is] the-service of [avodat] the-families of [mishpekhot] the-sons of [benei] the-Gershonites [ha-Gershunni] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed]; and-their-charge [u-mishmarttam] [shall be] in-the-hand of [be-yad] Ithamar [Itamar] the-son of [ben] Aaron [Aharon] the-priest [ha-kohen].

> **Key terms:** בְּיַד אִיתָמָר (be-yad Itamar) = "in the hand of Ithamar" — Ithamar oversees the Gershonites, while Eleazar (v. 16) oversees the Kohathites and the most sacred objects. The division of supervisory authority between Aaron's two surviving sons reflects a hierarchy of holiness.

---

## The Duties of the Merarites (vv. 29–33)

**29** The-sons of [benei] Merari [Merari]: by-their-families [le-mishpekhotam], by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam] you-shall-number [tifqod] them [otam].

**30** From-a-son of [mi-ben] thirty [sheloshim] year [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah] and-unto [ve-ad] a-son of [ben] fifty [khamishim] year [shanah] you-shall-number [tifqedem] them [otam], all [kol] who-come [ha-ba'] to-the-host [la-tsava'], to-do [la'avod] the-service of [et-avodat] the-tent of [ohel] meeting [mo'ed].

**31** And-this [ve-zo't] [is] the-charge of [mishmeret] their-carrying-load [massa'am] for-all [le-khol] their-service [avodatam] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed]: the-boards of [qershei] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan], and-its-bars [u-verikhav], and-its-pillars [ve-ammudav], and-its-sockets [va-adanav],

**32** and-the-pillars of [ve-ammudei] the-court [he-khatser] all-around [saviv] and-their-sockets [ve-adneihem], and-their-pegs [ve-yitedotam], and-their-cords [u-metareihem], for-all [le-khol] their-vessels [keleihem] and-for-all [u-le-khol] their-service [avodatam]; and-by-name [u-ve-shemot] you-shall-number [tifqedu] the-vessels of [et-kelei] the-charge of [mishmeret] their-carrying-load [massa'am].

> **Key terms:** קְרָשִׁים (qerashim) = "boards/frames" — the structural panels of the Tabernacle (Exod 26:15–30). בְּרִיחִים (berikhim) = "bars" — horizontal bars running through rings on the boards. עַמּוּדִים (ammudim) = "pillars/columns." אֲדָנִים (adanim) = "sockets/bases" — heavy silver pedestals. יְתֵדֹת (yetedot) = "pegs/stakes." The Merarites carry all the hard/structural components — the heaviest burden. וּבְשֵׁמֹת תִּפְקְדוּ (u-ve-shemot tifqedu) = "and by names you shall number" — each individual item is to be inventoried by name, preventing loss of critical structural pieces. **LXX:** κατ᾽ ὄνομα ἐπισκέψασθε αὐτά ("by name you shall inspect them").

**33** This [zo't] [is] the-service of [avodat] the-families of [mishpekhot] the-sons of [benei] Merari [Merari] for-all [le-khol] their-service [avodatam] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed], in-the-hand of [be-yad] Ithamar [Itamar] the-son of [ben] Aaron [Aharon] the-priest [ha-kohen].

> **Note:** Both the Gershonites (v. 28) and the Merarites are placed under Ithamar's oversight. Only the Kohathites, who carry the most sacred objects, serve under Eleazar.

---

## The Census of the Kohathites (vv. 34–37)

**34** And-numbered [va-yifqod] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] and-the-princes of [u-nesi'ei] the-congregation [ha-edah] the-sons of [et-benei] the-Kohathites [ha-Qehati] by-their-families [le-mishpekhotam] and-by-the-house of [u-le-veit] their-fathers [avotam],

**35** from-a-son of [mi-ben] thirty [sheloshim] year [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah] and-unto [ve-ad] a-son of [ben] fifty [khamishim] year [shanah], all [kol] who-come [ha-ba'] to-the-host [la-tsava'], for-service [la-avodah] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed].

**36** And-were [va-yihyu] their-numbered-ones [pequdeihem] by-their-families [le-mishpekhotam]: two-thousand [alpaim] seven [sheva'] hundred [me'ot] and-fifty [va-khamishim].

> **Census figure:** Kohathites aged 30–50: **two thousand seven hundred and fifty** (2,750).

**37** These [elleh] [are] the-numbered-ones of [pequdei] the-families of [mishpekhot] the-Kohathites [ha-Qehati], all [kol] who-serve [ha-oved] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed], whom [asher] numbered [paqad] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] by [al] the-mouth of [pi] YHWH, by-the-hand of [be-yad] Moses [Mosheh].

**38** And-the-numbered-ones of [u-pequdei] the-sons of [benei] Gershon [Gershon] by-their-families [le-mishpekhotam] and-by-the-house of [u-le-veit] their-fathers [avotam],

**39** from-a-son of [mi-ben] thirty [sheloshim] year [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah] and-unto [ve-ad] a-son of [ben] fifty [khamishim] year [shanah], all [kol] who-come [ha-ba'] to-the-host [la-tsava'], for-service [la-avodah] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed] —

> **Census figure:** Gershonites aged 30–50: **two thousand six hundred and thirty** (2,630).

**41** These [elleh] [are] the-numbered-ones of [pequdei] the-families of [mishpekhot] the-sons of [benei] Gershon [Gershon], all [kol] who-serve [ha-oved] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed], whom [asher] numbered [paqad] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] by [al] the-mouth of [pi] YHWH.

**43** from-a-son of [mi-ben] thirty [sheloshim] year [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah] and-unto [ve-ad] a-son of [ben] fifty [khamishim] year [shanah], all [kol] who-come [ha-ba'] to-the-host [la-tsava'], for-service [la-avodah] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed] —

**44** And-were [va-yihyu] their-numbered-ones [pequdeihem] by-their-families [le-mishpekhotam]: three-thousand [sheloshet alafim] and-two [u-matayim] hundred [me'ot].

**45** These [elleh] [are] the-numbered-ones of [pequdei] the-families of [mishpekhot] the-sons of [benei] Merari [Merari], whom [asher] numbered [paqad] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] by [al] the-mouth of [pi] YHWH, by-the-hand of [be-yad] Moses [Mosheh].

## The Grand Total (vv. 46–49)

**46** All [kol] the-numbered-ones [ha-pequdim] whom [asher] numbered [paqad] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] and-the-princes of [u-nesi'ei] Israel [Yisra'el] the-Levites [et-ha-Levi'im] by-their-families [le-mishpekhotam] and-by-the-house of [u-le-veit] their-fathers [avotam],

**47** from-a-son of [mi-ben] thirty [sheloshim] year [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah] and-unto [ve-ad] a-son of [ben] fifty [khamishim] year [shanah], all [kol] who-come [ha-ba'] to-do [la'avod] the-work of [avodat] service [avodah] and-the-work of [va-avodat] carrying-load [massa'] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed] —

**48** And-were [va-yihyu] their-numbered-ones [pequdeihem]: eight-thousand [shemonat alafim] five [va-khamesh] hundred [me'ot] and-eighty [u-shemonim].

> **Census figure:** Grand total of serving Levites aged 30–50: **eight thousand five hundred and eighty** (8,580). Verification: 2,750 (Kohathites) + 2,630 (Gershonites) + 3,200 (Merarites) = 8,580.

**49** By [al] the-mouth of [pi] YHWH he-numbered [paqad] them [otam] by-the-hand of [be-yad] Moses [Mosheh], each-man [ish] upon [al] his-service [avodato] and-upon [ve-al] his-carrying-load [massa'o]; and-his-numbered-ones [u-fequdav] as-which [ka'asher] commanded [tsivvah] YHWH [et] Moses [Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** The chapter closes by confirming that each Levite was assigned his specific task (עֲבֹדָה, 'avodah) and his specific burden (מַשָּׂא, massa') — individual accountability within communal service. **LXX:** ἕκαστον κατὰ τὴν αὐτοῦ λειτουργίαν ("each according to his own ministry").

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **The age of service — thirty vs. twenty-five (v. 3):** The MT consistently reads מִבֶּן שְׁלֹשִׁים שָׁנָה ("from a son of thirty years") throughout this chapter (vv. 3, 23, 30, 35, 39, 43, 47). The LXX Vaticanus, however, reads ἀπὸ εἰκοσιπέντε ἐτῶν ("from twenty-five years") in v. 3. This aligns with Num 8:24, where the MT itself specifies twenty-five years for Levitical service. The traditional harmonization (b. Hullin 24a) holds that Levites entered apprenticeship at twenty-five and began full carrying service at thirty. The EMET translation follows the Leningrad Codex reading of thirty.

2. **כְּבַלַּע — "as the swallowing" (v. 20):** This is one of the most debated phrases in the chapter. The root בלע means "to swallow/devour," but here it appears to denote a momentary glimpse — the instant of exposure when sacred objects are being covered. Rashi interprets it as "even for the time of swallowing" (i.e., the blink of an eye). Others read it as a substantive: "the dismantling/covering-up." The LXX renders the temporal sense with ἐξαίφνης ("suddenly"). The EMET translation preserves the Hebrew image: "as the swallowing of the holy things."

3. **תַּחַשׁ (takhash) — the mysterious covering (vv. 6, 8, 10, 11, 12, 14, 25):** This term appears seven times in the chapter as the outermost protective covering for sacred objects. Its identification remains uncertain. Ancient versions diverge: the LXX uses δέρμα ὑακίνθινον ("hyacinth-colored skin"), Aquila has σκιάδια ("canopies"), and the Vulgate reads ianthinas pelles ("violet skins"). The Talmud (b. Shabbat 28b) describes the takhash as a multicolored animal that existed only in Moses' time. Modern proposals include dugong, dolphin, fine goatskin, or a color-term rather than an animal species. The EMET translation transliterates takhash to preserve the ambiguity inherent in the Hebrew.

4. **The covering hierarchy — a theology of color:** The wrapping sequences encode a theology of holiness in color: (a) The Ark alone receives the parokhet veil first, then takhash skin, then a cloth entirely of blue (tekhelet) — blue outermost, because the Ark is the throne of the divine Presence. (b) The table, incense altar, and lampstand receive blue cloth innermost, then takhash skin outermost. (c) The bronze altar alone receives purple (argaman), not blue — possibly because it stands in the courtyard rather than the sanctuary. The Kohathites thus carry objects identifiable by their outer coverings: blue for the Ark, takhash for most vessels, purple for the bronze altar.

5. **The Kohathites' distinctive burden (v. 15):** Only the Kohathites receive the death warning (vv. 15, 18–20). They carry the holiest objects but may neither see them being covered (v. 20) nor touch them (v. 15). This paradox — greatest proximity to holiness with greatest danger — defines Kohathite identity. The word מַשָּׂא (massa', "burden/load") also means "oracle" in prophetic literature (cf. Isa 13:1), creating a rich double meaning: the Kohathites bear both physical weight and sacred responsibility.

6. **Itemized inventory — "by names" (v. 32):** The instruction וּבְשֵׁמוֹת תִּפְקְדוּ (u-ve-shemot tifqedu, "and by names you shall number") for the Merarites' equipment is unique in this chapter. No other clan receives this instruction. The structural components (boards, bars, pillars, sockets) are individually inventoried — presumably because each piece was fitted to a specific position and losing even one would compromise the Tabernacle's integrity.

7. **Census totals and proportionality:** The service-age census (30–50) yields 8,580 from a total of 22,000 Levites counted in chapter 3 (one month and upward) — roughly 39% of the total population. The Merarites have the largest service corps (3,200) despite being the smallest total clan (6,200 in 3:34), suggesting that the heaviest physical burden (structural components) required proportionally more workers. The Kohathites (2,750) and Gershonites (2,630) are comparatively close in number.

8. **LXX Vaticanus distinctive readings:** Beyond the age discrepancy in v. 3, the Vaticanus text at pa_0113–0114 shows the following noteworthy divergences: (a) ὑακίνθινον for תְּכֵלֶת throughout, conflating blue and takhash in some verses; (b) κατακάλυμμα ("covering") for פָּרֹכֶת in v. 5; (c) minor expansions clarifying pronoun referents. Overall, the LXX of Numbers 4 is a close and faithful rendering of a Vorlage very similar to the MT, with no major pluses or minuses.
